bullets.text: February CPI rose 0.3% m/m; annual inflation 2.4%. Shelter remained a key driver, keeping core inflation pressures elevated.
Real average hourly earnings rose 0.2% in February, suggesting modest wage gains after inflation and supporting consumer purchasing power.
Fed approved applications by Associated Banc-Corp and FirstSun Capital Bancorp, signaling continued regulatory greenlights for bank corporate actions.
Preliminary U.S. inquiry found the U.S. at fault in an Iran school strike, raising escalation risks and potential diplomatic fallout.
Multiple cargo ships were struck near Iran, including in the Strait of Hormuz, highlighting shipping disruption risk for global oil and trade flows.
IEA agreed to a record 400M-barrel release; U.S. planned 172M barrels from the SPR. Despite this, crude prices rose on supply-risk fears.
Equity futures fell sharply as oil climbed, with options markets pricing elevated tail risk from Iran conflict—raising recession/stagflation concerns.
Trump administration launched Section 301 trade probes into major partners (Mexico, China, EU and others), reviving tariff risk and supply-chain uncertainty.
U.S. deficit topped $1T through February but ran below last year’s pace, implying slightly improved near-term fiscal trajectory despite large borrowing needs.
India eased rules allowing Chinese investment after years of restrictions, signaling a potential thaw that could reshape regional capital flows and supply chains.
Google closed its acquisition of Wiz, reinforcing consolidation in cloud security amid rising enterprise cybersecurity spending and geopolitical hacking risks.
Atlassian cut roughly 10% of staff to self-fund AI and enterprise sales investments, reflecting broader tech reallocation from headcount toward AI capex.
Oracle shares jumped on strong results easing AI build-out concerns; filings showed a TikTok stake just over $2B, linking performance to major platform exposure.
Research suggested a massive ancient asteroid impact in the North Sea triggered a 330-foot tsunami, informing tail-risk assessments for coastal hazards.
Scientists reported a potential pill for sleep apnea, which could disrupt the large CPAP/device market if efficacy and safety hold in larger clinical trials.
